questions.prompt: What should the first deliverable be?

options.text: Electron desktop app (best FS + process spawning + clipboard)
Local web app (Node backend + browser UI)
Start as local web app, then package via Electron later

questions.prompt: How should the tool “run Codex” under the hood?

options.text: Spawn `codex` CLI as a subprocess and stream its output into the UI
Call an AI provider API directly (OpenAI/etc) and implement our own “skills” runner
Hybrid: planning via direct API, implementation via `codex` subprocess

answers.notes: Use the Codex SDK and CLI when needed.

questions.prompt: If spawning `codex`, should we assume it’s already installed on the machine?

options.text: Yes, external dependency is fine
No, bundle it / vendor it
Not sure yet

questions.prompt: Which “mode” and “permissions” do you want exposed in the UI?

options.text: Mirror Codex concepts (e.g. approval policy, sandbox mode, network access)
App-specific presets (Safe / Normal / Full access) that map to Codex settings internally
Both (advanced panel)

questions.prompt: Which model sources should be supported initially?

options.text: OpenAI only
OpenAI + Azure OpenAI
OpenAI + “any OpenAI-compatible” endpoint
Other:

questions.prompt: Where should codex-designer store its markdown artifacts by default?

options.text: In the target workspace under `docs/`
In a repo-local hidden folder (e.g. `.codex-designer/`) and optionally export to `docs/`
In a global folder (e.g. `~/.codex-designer/`) with links back to the repo

answers.notes: - also offer to run git init on a new folder

questions.prompt: Should codex-designer create `docs/` if missing, or ask first?

options.text: Create automatically
Ask / require confirmation per workspace

questions.prompt: Do you want the UI to enforce a hard cap of 10 rounds, or just warn?

options.text: Hard cap
Warn + allow override

questions.prompt: Should users be able to edit prior answers after moving to the next round?

options.text: Yes (with explicit “reopen round” and regenerate plan)
No (append-only, but allow new clarifications)
Yes, but only before implementation starts

questions.prompt: What does “Implement it from the interface” mean for v1?

options.text: One-button run that applies changes immediately
Run + show proposed diff(s) + require explicit “Apply” confirmation
Generate patch files only; user applies manually

answers.notes: Actually run codex with the $implement-plan skill on the plan file that has been generated as a result of the back and forth.
Use deterministic prompts (based on the skill guidance) so the app does not require the skill to be installed; still run via Codex SDK and keep `docs/[feature].impl.md` as the implementation log.

questions.prompt: During implementation runs, should codex-designer be allowed to run shell commands in the workspace?

options.text: Yes, but with step-level approvals shown in UI
Yes, no prompts (power-user)
No, file edits only

answers.notes: wrap codex sdk -- let codex do the actual work. don't try and implement codex from scratch, this is meant to be a layer on top to make it easier, not a full-fledged replacement. this allows users to use their own chatgpt logins, etc.

questions.prompt: How should “key features to test” be represented?

options.text: A markdown checklist file in `docs/` (e.g. `docs/[feature].test.md`)
A structured JSON file + an optional markdown export
Stored only in the app state (not ideal, but simplest)

answers.notes: A and B -- JSON should be edited and used as a mini-db --- markdown for user readability

questions.prompt: How should deferrals work?

options.text: Per test item, with a “defer until round N” field
Per test item, with freeform “blocked by …” notes and no fixed round number
Both

answers.notes: Just a checkbox to defer for that current round.

questions.prompt: Where should pasted images be stored?

options.text: Under the workspace (e.g. `docs/assets/[feature]/...`)
Under a repo-local app folder (e.g. `.codex-designer/attachments/...`)
Under a global app folder (e.g. `~/.codex-designer/attachments/...`)

questions.prompt: Any constraints we should enforce?

options.text: Max image size (MB): ___
Strip metadata (EXIF) on import: Yes/No
Allowed types: PNG/JPG/WebP/…

answers.notes: No restrictions for now.

questions.prompt: Any strong preferences for the UI stack?

options.text: React + Tailwind (+ Headless UI)
Vue + Tailwind
Svelte + Tailwind
Other:

questions.prompt: Icon set preference?

options.text: Heroicons
Lucide
Material Symbols
Other:

answers.notes: C (primary with A as a fallback)

questions.prompt: When you say “Codex SDK”, what should v1 do in practice?

options.text: Spawn `codex` CLI only (subprocess) and stream output
Use a Codex Node SDK if available; fallback to spawning the CLI
SDK only (no subprocess), even if it’s more work
Other: (describe expected interface)

answers.notes: -> B as a last resort

questions.prompt: Should codex-designer manage credentials at all?

options.text: No; rely entirely on Codex’s existing auth/config (user logs in outside the app)
Yes; allow setting API key / env vars per run profile in the UI
Both (default to A, with an advanced override)

questions.prompt: What exactly should be configurable in v1 “Advanced”?

options.text: approval policy, sandbox mode, network on/off, working directory, model
A + runtime limits (timeouts, max steps) and generation params (if supported)
Keep v1 minimal: model + 2–3 presets only

questions.prompt: What presets should we ship with by default (names + what they map to)?

answers.notes: Careful and YOLO.. best judgement on both --- YOLO should be no training wheels.

questions.prompt: Do you want a mandatory review step before changes land on disk?

options.text: Yes: require a clean git tree; run; show diff; user clicks “Apply”
Optional: show diff, but allow auto-apply
No: let Codex apply changes directly

questions.prompt: If the workspace is not a git repo, should codex-designer offer to `git init` before implementation runs?

options.text: Yes, always prompt
Yes, but only for “new project” workspaces
No

questions.prompt: If a user edits an earlier answer before implementation starts, what should happen?

options.text: “Reopen round”: regenerate plan starting from that round forward (keep an audit trail)
Append a correction note; only regenerate the next round
Disallow editing content; only allow new clarifications (despite earlier preference)

questions.prompt: For “defer this round”, should a deferred test automatically reappear in the next testing round?

options.text: Yes (and it’s “not evaluated” until you run it)
No (deferred stays deferred until manually un-deferred)
Support both behaviors per item

questions.prompt: Confirm these v1 paths (or propose alternatives):

answers.notes: Confirmed

questions.prompt: Which OSs should v1 target?

options.text: macOS only
macOS + Windows
macOS + Windows + Linux

questions.prompt: Preferred distribution format (if any): DMG / ZIP / installer / “dev only for now”?

answers.notes: dev only for now

questions.prompt: You said “best judgement” for presets — confirm these default mappings for v1?

options.text: `sandbox=workspace-write`, `approval=on-request`, `network=off`
`sandbox=workspace-write`, `approval=on-failure`, `network=off`
Other (describe)

questions.prompt: If the Codex SDK cannot support a requested setting (e.g. sandbox/network/approval) or cannot run the needed “skill flow”, how should v1 behave?

options.text: Transparently fall back to spawning the `codex` CLI for that run
Fall back, but show a warning + require explicit confirmation in the UI
Do not fall back; instead disable that capability in the UI until the SDK supports it

questions.prompt: Is it acceptable that run profiles + recent workspaces live in Electron `userData` (per-machine), while the actual artifacts live in the workspace `docs/`?

options.text: Yes
Prefer everything repo-local
Both: global defaults, but allow exporting/importing profiles per workspace

questions.prompt: Since there’s no mandatory pre-apply diff review, do you want an optional “checkpoint” before implementation runs?

options.text: Yes: offer “Create checkpoint commit” when git is available (default on in Careful)
Optional toggle only (default off)
No checkpoint support in v1

answers.notes: Default to commit locally before implementations. Autogen commit message based on changes (like on nano-model summary)

questions.prompt: For pre-implementation edits that become appended corrections (not rewriting history), how should the UI show the “current answer”?

options.text: Latest correction is the current answer; show history collapsed/expandable
Original answer stays “current”; corrections are notes only
Other (describe)

answers.notes: Append to the end of the doc in the current round -- pull it forward preserve the previous round to show the evolution of the thought process around the concept.

questions.prompt: How should `docs/[feature].test.json` track testing rounds?

options.text: Store only current status per test, plus a lightweight per-round summary history
Store full per-round results (each round has statuses/notes/attachments)
No history; latest only

questions.prompt: Confirm statuses needed per test item (v1):

options.text: `not_run`, `pass`, `fail`, `deferred`, `blocked`
`pass`, `fail`, `deferred` only (keep it minimal)
Other (describe)

answers.notes: plus feedback lines (text+image)

questions.prompt: When you click “Implement feedback” from failed tests, should codex-designer:

options.text: Run implementation only (patch code) using failing tests/notes as input; do not rewrite the plan
Update the plan first (so plan stays source of truth), then run implementation
Ask each time (A/B)

answers.notes: Resume implementation session and add feedback to the prompt with test context

questions.prompt: In presets/advanced settings, what should “network=off” actually mean?

options.text: Disallow network for tool/executed commands (e.g. `npm install`, `curl`), but model calls still work
Fully offline (no model calls / no installs) — only local edits
Other (describe)

questions.prompt: For the default pre-implementation checkpoint commit, what should happen if the git working tree is dirty?

options.text: Commit all current changes (staging automatically) as the checkpoint
Prompt user to choose what to include (stage/unstage UI)
Refuse and ask user to clean up manually

questions.prompt: If the working tree is clean, should we create an empty checkpoint commit anyway?

options.text: Yes (for a consistent undo anchor)
No (just remember the current HEAD as the checkpoint)

questions.prompt: After an implementation run finishes, should codex-designer also offer to auto-commit the new changes?

options.text: Yes (default on in Careful, off in YOLO)
Yes (default on in both)
No (checkpoint only)

answers.notes: commit happens after acceptance/testing.

questions.prompt: When exporting run profiles per workspace, where should the exported file live?

options.text: `docs/.codex-designer/profiles.json`
`.codex-designer/profiles.json` (repo root)
`docs/codex-designer.profiles.json`
Other:

questions.prompt: For “resume implementation session”, should sessions be resumable across app restarts?

options.text: Yes (store a stable session/run identifier)
No (resume only within the current app session)
Best effort (resume when possible; otherwise start a new session)

answers.notes: C (prefer A)

questions.prompt: When a correction is appended, should codex-designer automatically regenerate the plan (from the next round), or require an explicit action?

options.text: Auto-regenerate immediately
Require clicking “Regenerate plan”
Ask each time

answers.notes: should be part of the Q&A rounds

questions.prompt: In the UI, how should the “current answer” vs history be displayed?

options.text: Show current answer (latest), with a collapsible “Answer history” timeline
Always show the full evolution inline (no collapse)
Other:

answers.notes: but expanded by default

questions.prompt: What should start a new “testing round” in `docs/[feature].test.json`?

options.text: Each time the user clicks “Start testing round”
Each time after an implementation run completes
Both (A is manual; B auto-creates a new round if tests change)

questions.prompt: For feedback lines (text + images), should we store them:

options.text: On the test item overall (shared across rounds)
Per-round, per-test item (recommended)
Per-failure only (when status = fail)

questions.prompt: Should `.codex-designer/` be gitignored by default?

options.text: Yes, add to `.gitignore` automatically (profiles are local-only)
No, keep it commit-friendly by default (teams can share profiles)
Ask per workspace (“Local only” vs “Shareable in repo”)

questions.prompt: What should “acceptance” mean for enabling a final commit?

options.text: All “key features to test” are `pass` (no fails/blocked), then allow “Commit accepted changes”
Manual “Mark accepted” toggle independent of test statuses
Both (A gates by default in Careful; YOLO allows manual override)

questions.prompt: When the user clicks “Commit accepted changes”, should codex-designer:

options.text: Stage and commit all changes in the repo
Show a simple file list for inclusion/exclusion (no full staging UI)
Require the user to stage manually outside the app

questions.prompt: For “auto-create a new testing round after implementation runs when tests change”, what should count as “tests change” in v1?

options.text: Any implementation run that modifies files in the workspace
Only if the test definition list changes (`docs/[feature].test.json` items added/removed/edited)
Only if there were code changes AND at least one test is not `pass`
Other:

questions.prompt: In Careful preset, should the UI allow a one-shot override for a single run (e.g. temporarily enable tool-network for `npm install`)?

options.text: Yes (explicit per-run override with clear warning)
No (must change the active profile)
Yes, but only via Advanced panel

questions.prompt: If we cannot truly “resume” a prior Codex session (SDK limitation), should codex-designer:

options.text: Start a new session but include a structured recap + links to artifacts/run logs as context
Start a new session with no recap (clean slate)
Block and require SDK resume support

questions.prompt: When Q&A has new answers/corrections that haven’t been folded into the plan yet, should codex-designer block implementation until “Regenerate plan” runs?

options.text: Yes (plan must be source of truth before implementation)
No (allow implementation but warn)
Only block in Careful; YOLO can proceed

questions.prompt: Since you want to choose “Local only” vs “Shareable in repo” per workspace, should we split `.codex-designer/` into:

options.text: `.codex-designer/share/` (profiles, committed if shareable) + `.codex-designer/cache/` (always gitignored: run logs, snapshots, etc.)
Keep a single folder and just toggle gitignore on/off for the whole thing
Other (describe)

questions.prompt: Regardless of shareability, should run logs (stream output, diffs, checkpoints metadata) be kept:

options.text: Repo-local under `.codex-designer/cache/`
Only in Electron `userData`
Both (cache in repo for portability; userData for recent/perf)

questions.prompt: In Careful preset, should “All tests pass” mean:

options.text: Every key test status is `pass` (so `deferred`/`blocked`/`not_run` prevent acceptance)
`pass` or `deferred` is acceptable (allow acceptance with deferred items)
Other (describe)

questions.prompt: You want “stage + commit all changes”. If there are still failing/unrun tests, should YOLO allow committing anyway?

options.text: Yes (manual override)
No (commit action always gated by acceptance)

questions.prompt: Should the commit message be:

options.text: Auto-generated but editable before commit
Fully auto (no edit)
Manual entry only (no generator)

questions.prompt: To enforce “plan must be source of truth” in Careful, where should we store the “plan generated from Q&A up to round N / hash” metadata?

options.text: In `.codex-designer/cache/state.json` only (no changes to docs)
As a small machine-readable footer/header in the plan/Q&A markdown (portable across machines)
Both

questions.prompt: When we auto-create a new testing round after code changes (and some tests aren’t `pass`), how should the new round initialize statuses?

options.text: Copy previous round statuses forward as defaults
Reset all to `not_run` (previous round remains as history)
Reset only previously-`pass` tests to `not_run` (keep fail/deferred/blocked)

answers.notes: You choose.

questions.prompt: Which settings should be eligible for a one-shot override in Careful?

options.text: Network only (tool/executed commands)
Network + approval policy
Network + approval + sandbox
Other (describe)

answers.notes: I don't care. Just choose something that makes sense. I'll be using YOLO all the time anyway.
